ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 300–650L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Nothing Traveled Alone

STS-MS6-L1-300650-MSSCI6-MEC-IMR-01 • MS Science Grade 6 • Body Systems as Interacting Subsystems
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Museums usually decide for you what a thing means. This one does not. Amara, Bilal, and Nadia are curating their own exhibit, and they chose suitcases on purpose, because every family in this neighborhood arrived carrying one. Inside each suitcase is a different body system, and the students are showing that a body, like a family, only works when every part travels together.
If one suitcase went missing, which system would the body miss the fastest, and why?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Amara doing?
2
What is Bilal holding?
3
What is the teacher doing?
4
What place is this? How do you know?
5
Tell me about the exhibit table.
6
What is Nadia wearing, and what is she carrying?
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
